data.title: Qflow Group AB (publ) year-end report January - December 2024
data.description:
October - December 2024

  • Net sales increased 65% to SEK 297 (180) million
  • Organically, sales increased 13%
  • EBITA increased 31% to SEK 47 (36) million
  • Pro forma net sales increased 9% to SEK 308 (283) million
  • Pro forma adjusted EBITDA increased 4% to SEK 58 (56) million
  • Pro forma adjusted EBITA increased 2% to SEK 56 (55) million
  • Expanded presence in Sweden: Qflow further strengthened its position in the Swedish market through the acquisition of Moresco Projektledning AB in December 2024
  • Loan facilities were settled in October, and a Revolving Credit Facility (RCF), amounted to SEK 150 million, was signed.

Comment from the CEO

A steady fourth quarter

Pro forma net sales and earnings increased in the quarter compared to last year. The slower start to the year in the first quarter resulted in slightly lower net sales growth for the full year. The full year EBITA was impacted by the first quarter's weaker performance, and we observed a decrease in attendance rate during the fourth quarter due to a higher volume of vacation compared to last year.

The overall demand for Qflow’s service is at a stable level. While certain market segments faced challenges due to reduced demand and pricing pressures, the company successfully secured new contracts for several large-scale infrastructure and construction projects. These wins, coupled with our ability to scale our teams to meet project demands, have positioned us well for future growth. Our assignments in long-term infrastructure and construction projects are progressing as planned, and we have successfully onboarded additional consultants to support our customers.

The Group expanded its presence in the market with the acquisition of Moresco Projektledning AB (in December), which specializes in providing operational management and project support for infrastructure projects. This brings the total number of companies within the Group to 19 in Sweden and Norway.

data.title: Conversion of shares in Viaplay Group
data.description:
Viaplay Group

According to Viaplay Group AB’s (publ) (”Viaplay”) articles of association, owners of Class A shares have the right to have such shares converted into Class B shares. Conversion reduces the total number of votes in the company. When such a conversion has occurred, Viaplay is obliged under the Swedish Financial Instruments Trading Act (1991:980) to disclose any such change.

During February 2025, at the request of a shareholder, 120,008 Class A shares were converted to Class B shares. The total number of votes in the company thereafter amounts to 4,582,825,996.

The total number of registered shares in the company amounts to 4,579,122,244 shares of which 411,528 are Class A shares, 4,577,821,216 are Class B shares and 889,500 are Class C shares.

****

NOTES TO EDITORS

Viaplay Group AB (publ) is the Nordic region’s leading entertainment provider. Our Viaplay streaming service is available in every Nordic country, as well as in the Netherlands and Poland, and our Viaplay Select branded content concept has been added to partner platforms around the world. We also operate TV channels across most of our markets, as well as radio stations in Norway and Sweden. Our talented people come to work every day with a shared passion and clear mission to entertain millions of people with our unique offering of locally relevant storytelling, which spans premium live sports, films, series and music. Our purpose is to grow our business profitably and responsibly, and deliver sustainable value for all our stakeholders. Viaplay Group is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (‘VPLAY B’).

This information is such that Viaplay Group AB (publ) must disclose in accordance with the Swedish Financial Instruments Trading Act. The information was submitted for publication on 28 February 2025, at 10:00 CET.

Contact us:

Email: press@viaplaygroup.com | investors@viaplaygroup.com

Phone: Anna Hedenberg, Head of Investor Relations & Interim Head of Corporate Communications: +46 73 699 21 48 (anna.hedenberg@viaplaygroup.com)

Follow us: viaplaygroup.com / LinkedIn /

data.title: NBSX: PNH - Trading Statement
data.description:
=====================Trading Statement

PARATUS NAMIBIA HOLDINGS LIMITED(Incorporated in the Republic of Namibia)(Registration number 2017/0558)NSX Share code: PNHISIN code: NA 000A2DTQ42("Paratus" or "the Company")

TRADING STATEMENT IN RESPECT OF THE UNAUDITED RESULTS FOR THE PERIODENDED 31 DECEMBER 2024

In compliance with the Namibian Securities Exchange ('NSX') Listings Requirements, ParatusNamibia Holdings Limited ('Paratus') shareholders are advised of the following in relation to the31 December 2024 interim results:

1. Headline earnings per ordinary share (HEPS) are anticipated to be substantially lower (greater than 30%) in relation to the 31 December 2023 comparative period; 2. Basic earnings per ordinary share (BEPS) are anticipated to be substantially lower (greater than 30%) in relation to the 31 December 2023 comparative period; 3. Revenue for the period is anticipated to be materially higher (between 10% and 30%) in relation to the 31 December 2023 comparative period; and 4. Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) for the period is anticipated to be significantly higher (less than 10%) in relation to the 31 December 2023 comparative period.

The decrease in HEPS and BEPS can be ascribed to the increase in the number of ordinary sharesin issue, when compared to the 31 December 2023 period, following the rights issue completed inJune 2024. At the end of the reporting period, the proceeds from the rights issue were still in theprocess of being utilised for the deployment of revenue producing infrastructure. Revenue fromthis infrastructure is only expected to materialise, and gradually ramp up from the second half ofthe 2025 financial year, as new customers join the expanded network.

This trading statement has not been reviewed or reported on by Paratus' external auditors. Theunaudited results for the period ended 31 December 2024 are expected to be published on or about14 March 2025.

By order of the Board28 February 2025

SponsorSimonis Storm Securities (Pty) LtdMember of the Namibia Stock Exchange4 Koch Street, Klein WindhoekP.O. Box 3970, Windhoek

Date: 28-02-2025 11:00:00Produced by the JSE SENS Department. The SENS service is an information dissemination service administered by the JSE Limited ('JSE'). data.title: Hungary Producer Inflation Rises Further
data.description:
Hungary’s annual producer inflation edged up to 9.1% in January 2025 from 9% in the previous month.

This marked the eight straight month of rising producer prices and the highest reading since May 2023, primarily driven by higher prices for mining & quarrying (13% vs 10.1% in December).

On the other hand, price growth slowed for manufacturing (7.3% vs 7.4%) and electricity, gas, steam & air-conditioning supply (10.8% vs 12.6%).

Meanwhile, prices increased further for domestic output (6.1% vs 4%), while rose at a softer pace for non-domestic output (10.6% vs 11.5%).

On a monthly basis, producer prices rose by 0.4% in January, following a 0.3% increase in December.

data.title: WPP shares extend losses after bleak annual outlook; analysts slash PT
data.description:
** Shares of ad group WPP down 1.7% to 634.20p; set for its third straight session of declines, if losses hold

** Co on Thursday said it expects revenue and profit to be flat at best this year because of a weak Chinese market and uncertainty in the United States

** Also posted 1.0% fall in organic revenue, missing analysts forecasts of 0.4% drop

** At least seven brokerages, including Morgan Stanley and Deutsche Bank, cut PT on stock following results - data compiled by LSEG

** Barclays downgrades WPP to "equal-weight" from "overweight" and cuts PT to 780p from 975p, citing "sorely lacking" momentum and missed growth targets

** Brokerage is worried that co's prolonged turnaround and stagnant organic growth signal deeper issues, casting doubt on its ability to meet 2026 growth targets

** Up to Thursday's close, stock has dipped ~22% YTD

data.title: French Stocks Extend Fall Amid Tariff Fears
data.description:
The CAC 40 dropped 0.5% to 8,060 on Friday, extending losses from the previous session as market sentiment remained subdued amid escalating tariff concerns.

US President Donald Trump suggested that the U.S. and the UK could strike a trade deal free of tariffs, while also hinting earlier this week at the imminent announcement of new levies on the European Union.

Meanwhile, investors processed a fresh batch of corporate earnings results alongside key economic data from France.

Notably, preliminary estimates revealed that the annual inflation rate slowed significantly to a four-year low of 0.8% in February 2025 from 1.7% in the previous month and below market expectations of 1%.

Among individual stocks, Teleperformance was the biggest loser, with shares dropping 12% despite reporting higher revenue and a positive 2025 outlook.

In contrast, Saint-Gobain rose 1.9% after reporting higher FY24 net income.

Over the month, the index is on track for its fifth consecutive monthly gain.

data.title: Supervisory Board extends contract for Chief Financial Officer Tobias Ohler until 2030
data.description:
Wacker Chemie AG

by Manuela Dollinger

Business news for the stock market

Wacker Chemie AG: Supervisory Board extends contract for Chief Financial Officer Tobias Ohler until 2030

Munich (pta000/28.02.2025/10:00 UTC+1)

Munich, February 28, 2025 – In its meeting yesterday, the Supervisory Board of Wacker Chemie AG re-appointed Tobias Ohler (54) for another five years as CFO. A member of the WACKER Executive Board since 2013, Ohler is currently under a contract that is set to expire December 31, 2025.

"Tobias Ohler does an outstanding job for WACKER. Extending his contract honors the lasting impact of his work. We are pleased to continue our successful, trusting collaboration with him," noted Peter-Alexander Wacker, chairman of the Wacker Chemie AG Supervisory Board, in his comments on the decision.

Dr. Tobias Ohler studied economics, business administration and industrial engineering in France, Germany and Australia, and earned his doctorate in economics at the University of Oldenburg. Following his studies, he was a consultant at McKinsey from 1997 until 2004. He joined Wacker Chemie AG in 2004 as head of Controlling. In 2008, he assumed the leadership of Raw Materials Procurement and, in 2010, joined the Executive Board of Siltronic AG. He has been on the Executive Board of Wacker Chemie AG since January 2013.

For further information, please contact:

Wacker Chemie AG

Media Relations

Manuela Dollinger

Tel. +49 89 6279-1629

Manuela.Dollinger@wacker.com

(end)

data.title: Fitch Affirms Tata Chemicals Issuer Default Rating at BB+; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings affirmed its BB+ long-term issuer default rating on Tata Chemicals , according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

The rating outlook is stable.

data.title: Proximus results and forecasts 'provide a bit of relief', shares rise
data.description:
** Shares in Proximus rise 3% to December highs after the Belgian telecoms group published its Q4 results and forecast more profit growth in 2025

** New residential mobile customers in Q4 stand at 27,000, offsetting no-frills brand Digi's arrival in Belgium, while group profit is expected to grow around 2% in 2025 with Proximus Global at +20%

** "We think this is a rather neutral set of results and guidance but it could be a bit of a relief," ING analyst David Vagman says

** The company gave reassuring words about Digi's impact so far and the 2025 outlook, he adds

** Degroof Petercam also flags benefits of cost savings plans both at domestic and international activities, which are expected to reassure investors

** Proximus shares are up 17% this year as of Thursday's session close, recovering slightly from a 78% drop since December 2019

data.title: International Paper Appoints Lance Loeffler Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
International Paper said Friday it appointed Lance Loeffler chief financial officer.

Loeffler, who has served in finance and leadership roles at UBS Investment Bank, Deutsche Bank Securities and Halliburton (HAL), is set to succeed Tim Nicholls, who was named president of International Paper company DS Smith.

Nicholls has been CFO of International Paper since 2018.

data.title: Jefferies Downgrades Kakaku.com to Hold From Buy, Adjusts Price Target to 2,320 Yen From 2,770 Yen
data.description: Kakaku.com has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from 2,320 yen to 3,000 yen, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Rightmove's Pressure to Deliver Mounts — Market Talk
data.description: Rightmove can't afford to sit still after it rebuffed Australian real-estate advertiser REA's approach and peer OnTheMarket was acquired by CoStar, Wealth Club's Charlie Huggins says. The property website needs to step up innovation instead of relying on big price increases for growth as the pressure is on for it to deliver, Huggins writes in a note. Although operating margins are no longer rising, shareholders won't mind as long as this accelerates top-line growth, he says. Meanwhile, strategic growth areas like rental services show good early traction, he adds. "Rightmove still retains a very dominant position...However, with CoStar snapping at its heels, it certainly cannot afford to rest on its laurels." Shares are up 2% at 655.40 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0855 GMT - Rightmove can't afford to sit still after it rebuffed Australian real-estate advertiser REA's approach and peer OnTheMarket was acquired by CoStar, Wealth Club's Charlie Huggins says. The property website needs to step up innovation instead of relying on big price increases for growth as the pressure is on for it to deliver, Huggins writes in a note. Although operating margins are no longer rising, shareholders won't mind as long as this accelerates top-line growth, he says. Meanwhile, strategic growth areas like rental services show good early traction, he adds. "Rightmove still retains a very dominant position...However, with CoStar snapping at its heels, it certainly cannot afford to rest on its laurels." Shares are up 2% at 655.40 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0850 GMT - IAG is benefiting from cheaper fuel prices and resilient demand for travel, which helped boost its operating profit and its share price to five-year highs, Begbies Traynor partner Julie Palmer says in a note to clients. IAG appears to be defying the gloom weighing on consumer-facing sectors in the U.K., the analyst says. The owner of British Airways, Iberia and Vueling posted an operating profit before exceptional items of 4.44 billion euros last year. "Despite current successes, IAG must remain wary of the potential headwinds of a volatile geopolitical climate, the possible impact of tariffs and a highly competitive travel market," the analyst says. IAG shares trade up 3.9% at 351.90 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0844 GMT - Allianz's guidance for 2025 might trigger some share weakness as it is below consensus expectations, but the company tends to achieve the higher end of its outlook range throughout the year, Deutsche Bank's Rhea Shah and Amalie Zdravkovic say in a research note. Europe's largest insurer said it expects an operating profit of 16 billion euros this year, plus or minus 1 billion euros. The mid-point of this range is below both Deutsche Bank's estimate of 16.5 billion euros and the 16.7 billion euros anticipated by the consensus, the analysts say. Allianz's shares have done well into the results this week, so there could be some weakness due to the company's guidance, Deutsche Bank says. Shares trade 0.5% lower, but are up 3.6% week to date. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - Weir Group's full-year 2024 results were boosted by accelerating demand in its aftermarket business and a strong operational performance over the second half of the year, Shore Capital analysts Akhil Patel and Tom Fraine write. Weir also announced the acquisition of mining software solutions company Micromine for 657 million pounds. The business should contribute around 34 million pounds in revenue in 2025, the analysts write. It is expected to make an earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization contribution of 16.5 million pounds, they add. Shares trade up 0.35% at 22.94 pounds. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - Hong Kong shares ended lower after President Trump announced an additional 10% tariff on Chinese imports, spooking investors. Trump cited China's role in the fentanyl trade as the main reason for the new tariffs, which led investors to turn to de-risking, IG market strategist Yeap Jun Rong said. The benchmark Hang Seng Index closed 3.3% lower at 22941.32, marking its largest one-day loss since October 2024. The Hang Seng Tech Index dropped 5.3%. Nearly all sectors lost ground, with BYD Electronic leading the losses, falling 10.5%. Longfor Group dropped 8.9%, Li Auto and Geely Automobile fell 8.1% and 8.0%, respectively. Among the few gainers, Galaxy Entertainment rose 1.9% and Sands China gained 0.45%.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0822 GMT - Volvo's move to re-hire workers at its largest plant in Europe probably confirms a recovery of the market, Deutsche Bank analyst Nicolai Kempf writes. In North America, the bank understands that the company will continue to build its new plant in Mexico, despite potential tariffs risks. "Overall, we remain constructive on the name, and given a potential recovery of the end markets we increase our target multiples and lift our target price to 360 Swedish kronor from 310 kronor." The bank reiterates its buy rating. Shares trade 1.4% lower at 334.50 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0818 GMT - Bitcoin falls to a three-and-a-half-month low as fresh concerns over U.S. tariffs dent risk appetite. President Trump said Thursday he would press ahead with a 25% tariff on Canadian and Mexican imports on March 4 and impose an additional 10% tariff on Chinese goods. Risk aversion and significant outflows from bitcoin-backed exchange traded funds have intensified selling pressure, Zaye Capital Markets analyst Naeem Aslam says in a note. The recent ByBit crypto exchange hack has also shaken confidence, he says. "Investors are advised to monitor these developments closely, as ongoing macroeconomic policies and security issues within the cryptocurrency space may continue to impact Bitcoin's volatility in the near term." Bitcoin falls to a low of $78,813, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0817 GMT - Forvia hasn't proposed a dividend for 2024 to prioritize debt reduction, as anticipated, but its guidance for 2025 looks weaker than expected, Bernstein analysts say. "The mid-point of Forvia's 2025 outlook is below the current consensus expectations on revenue and operating profit which we think will be taken negatively, especially in the context of [peer] Valeo's results and more upbeat outlook last night," the analysts say. The French auto-parts supplier expects 2025 revenue to be between 26.3 billion euros and 27.5 billion euros and guides for an operating margin of between 5.2% and 6.0%. Its results for last year were broadly in line with consensus expectations, and cash flow surprised positively, Bernstein says. Shares fall 17%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0817 GMT - Weir Group's full-year earnings came in line with expectations, J.P.Morgan analysts Lushanthan Mahendrarajah and Jeremy Caspar write. The U.K. engineering company reported a strong adjusted operating margin of 18.8% which beat consensus expectations of 18.2%, the analysts write. This offset a 3% miss to revenue expectations, they add. The group updated its savings target to 80 million pounds in 2026 and increased its operating margin target to beyond 20%, up from previous guidance of 20%, they write. The company has a credible roadmap to meet its margin target, they add. Shares trade up 0.35% at 22.94 pounds. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0758 GMT - Ferrovial lifted its shareholder-return target disclosing a new buyback of 500 million euros, but this probably won't be enough to support the shares, JPMorgan analysts say. The European infrastructure group now plans to return 2.2 billion euros for the 2024-26 period, up from 1.7 billion euros previously. In light of the 2 billion euros in cash it received last year from the sale of a stake in London's Heathrow Airport, the additional buyback might fall short, the analysts say. Ferrovial's fourth-quarter adjusted Ebitda of 334 million euros missed consensus expectations by 9.4%, with both construction and toll roads weaker than hoped, JPM says. This and limited details on the 2025 outlook could trigger cuts to consensus expectations, the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0749 GMT - Aztech Global's outlook remains uncertain, UOB Kay Hian analysts say, as the brokerage lowers the stock's target price to S$0.65 from S$0.90 while maintaining a hold rating. The electronics manufacturer's 2024 earnings were slightly below expectations, the analysts write in a note. They say the company is experiencing some volatility in customer demand amid rising trade restrictions and global economic uncertainties. UOB KH lowers its 2025 and 2026 revenue forecasts by 26% and 25%, respectively, given the lower-than-expected results and an uncertain outlook. Shares are 0.7% higher at S$0.76. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0746 GMT - European stocks are expected to open lower as concerns over global trade tensions weigh. IG futures data show the DAX falling 1.1%, the CAC 40 dropping 0.9% and the FTSE 100 down 0.8% at the opening bell. President Trump took to his Truth Social platform Thursday to confirm that he would implement a 25% tariff on Canadian and Mexican imports on March 4. An additional 10% tariff on Chinese goods will be imposed on the same date. This announcement has "raised fears of retaliatory measures, further disrupting global supply chains and corporate earnings," Zaye Capital Markets analyst Naeem Aslam says in a note. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: German unemployment rises less than forecast in February, labour office says
data.description:
The number of people out of work in Germany rose in February but at a slower rate than expected, federal labour office figures showed on Friday.

The office said the number of unemployed increased by 5,000 in seasonally adjusted terms to 2.89 million. Analysts polled by Reuters had expected that figure to rise by 15,000.

The seasonally adjusted job rate remained unchanged on the previous month at 6.2%, in line with a forecast of analysts polled by Reuters.

"The economic weakness remains visible on the labour market in February," said the labour office's Daniel Terzenbach.

Smarting from two consecutive years of decline in 2023 and 2024, Europe's largest economy is still battling with persistent weakness and structural headwinds in industry.

With these challenges weighing on the job market, the number of unemployed people in Germany could surpass the three million mark for the first time in 10 years this year.

On Wednesday, the Ifo economic institute said German companies are planning to reduce their headcounts across sectors, with the most severe job cuts seen in manufacturing, according to a February survey.

data.title: Germany February unemployment change 5k vs 15k expected
data.description:
• Prior 11k
  • Unemployment rate 6.2% vs 6.2% expected
  • Prior 6.2%

data.title: Amadeus IT Group to Launch EUR1 Billion Share Repurchase
data.description:
Amadeus IT Group board agreed to launch a share buyback program of up to 1.3 billion euros to reduce its share capital.

The 12-month program comprises the repurchase of a maximum of 19,231,000 shares, or 4.27% of the Spanish technology company's share capital, according to a Friday release.

The reduction in share capital via the bought-back shares is subject to approval at the ordinary general meeting of shareholders.

data.title: Sanxin Medtec Signs Deal With Industrial Park Committee for Planned Equipment Production Base
data.description:
Jiangxi Sanxin Medtec signed a cooperation agreement with the Nanchang Xiaolan Economic and Technological Development Zone Management Committee to obtain rights and approvals for the planned production base for blood purification equipment.

The medical device maker will invest 500 million yuan into the project, which was signed in September 2024, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The company's shares closed less than 2% lower.

data.title: Live Verdure Posts HY Revenues From Ordinary Activities Of A$1.1 Million
data.description:
Live Verdure Ltd :

  • HY REVENUES FROM ORDINARY ACTIVITIES A$1.1 MILLION

  • PROFIT FOR HALF-YEAR ATTRIBUTABLE A$77.1 MILLION

data.title: Market Chatter: Schroders CEO Said to Unveil Overhaul Plans in March
data.description:
Schroders Chief Executive Officer Richard Oldfield is expected to present a plan to revamp the asset manager on March 6, Reuters reported Friday, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The plan is expected to include cost reductions and a prioritization of growth areas, including wealth and private markets. The expected overhaul follows performance pressure from the company's founding family, which holds a 44% stake, the report said.

Schroders did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Bekaert slides after 'weak' 2025 guidance
data.description:
** Shares in Bekaert fall as much as 3.5% after the Belgian steel wire maker posted in-line full-year results and a cautious guidance for 2025

** The company expects the weak business environment of H2 2024 to persist into 2025 and guides for flat revenues and "at least stable" margins for this year

** ING says the new guidance suggests weak H1 2025 momentum

** "Bekaert guides for flat sales and at least flat uEBIT margins in 2025, which seems slightly more conservative than our and consensus forecasts", KBC analysts say

** The group's FY revenue and EBIT declined 9% and 10% respectively

data.title: India's BSE suffers biggest monthly fall in over 2 years
data.description:
** Shares of stock exchange co BSE Ltd fall about 13% in February, its biggest monthly drop since February 2023

** Stock falls 11% on Friday to 4,587 rupees, lowest since early Dec 2024

** Reuters could not immediately ascertain the reason behind the move

** Analysts, on average, rate BSEL "buy", with median PT of 6,100 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock down ~3.1% YTD

data.title: S.Korea, Indonesia weigh on Asian currencies, stocks as Trump tariffs loom
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indonesian rupiah hits near 5-year low
  • Thai baht set for worst monthly drop since November
  • Thai, Indonesian stocks set for worst month since March 2020

Indonesia and South Korea led a downturn in Asian currencies and stocks on Friday as the U.S. dollar strengthened and markets prepared for President Donald Trump's impending tariffs on China, Mexico, and Canada.

The Korean won > lost as much as 1.3% to a three-week low of 1,463.9 against the U.S. dollar. Equities in Seoul dropped as much as 3.4% and headed for their worst day since August.

The MSCI gauge of emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) lost 0.3%, while a gauge of emerging Asian equities (.MIMS00000PUS) dropped 1%.

Overnight, Trump said his proposed 25% tariffs on Mexican and Canadian goods will take effect on March 4 along with an extra 10% duty on Chinese imports, sapping expectations for a further delay in their implementation.

That prompted the dollar index to hit a two-week peak, against a basket of currencies.

"Markets are now thinking what is next. After tariffs on China, Mexico and Canada, tariffs on products such as cars and semiconductors may materialise," said Jeff Ng, head of Asia macro strategy at Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation.

Such tariffs will negatively impact the currencies of trade-open economies such as South Korea, Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand, Ng said.

The Indonesian rupiah dropped 0.8% to its lowest level since March 2020 and was headed for its fifth consecutive monthly loss. Shares in Jakarta fell 2.5% and were down 11.1% for February, their worst monthly performance since March 2020.

The Thai baht dropped 0.2% and was set for its biggest monthly drop since November, following a surprise rate cut from the central bank.

The Philippine peso was flat but was 0.8% higher for the month. The Philippine central bank had unexpectedly kept its key interest rate steady earlier this month.

Among other regional equity markets, Thai stocks fell 1.6% and headed for their biggest monthly drop since March 2020.

Malaysian shares lost 0.7%, with the central bank's monetary policy decision next week on investors' radar.

Major ASEAN indexes are likely to continue to be volatile next week as the additional U.S. duties on China, Southeast Asia's biggest trading partner, come into effect, said Jason Kuan, director of Investment Research and Advisory at CIMB Singapore.

Beijing said it opposed Trump's latest tariff threat and promised retaliation to protect its interests.

Chinese stocks closed 2% lower. The National People's Congress meeting next week will be in focus, with investors expecting additional stimulus amid Trump's trade policies.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** India's economic growth expected to have picked-up in the October to December quarter

** Thai factory output falls 0.85 y/y in January, better than forecast

** China's manufacturing activity set to contract for second month in Feb

data.title: Pool Insider Sold Shares Worth $9,581,250, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
De La Mesa Manuel J Perez, Director, on February 25, 2025, sold 27,500 shares in Pool for $9,581,250. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Perez has control over a total of 916,679 common shares of the company, with 62,577 shares held directly and 854,102 controlled indirectly.
